name: Sugar-Cookie Cutouts
recipeIngredient: White, green and/or yellow ready-to-pipe icing
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter and sugar in a large bowl with an electric mixer until pale and fluffy. Beat in eggs and vanilla until blended.
2. With the mixer on low speed, gradually beat in flour, baking powder, and cream until well combined.
3. Press dough together, then divide into two halves. Wrap each half separately and chill for 3 hours or until firm enough to roll.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease cookie sheets as needed.
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll one piece of dough at a time (keep the remainder refrigerated) to a thickness of 1/4 inch. Cut into desired shapes using floured 3-inch cookie cutters. Sprinkle each shape with half a teaspoon of colored sugar. Place the cookies about an inch apart on prepared cookie sheets.
6. Roll any remaining scraps and chill, then repeat step 5 with the remaining dough.
7. Bake for 14 minutes or until the bottoms and edges are lightly browned. Allow to cool completely on wire racks.
8. Decorate as desired. Let stand two hours until icing sets. Store airtight up to 2 weeks or freeze.
